About the program

The business of law — built for practitioners, not students.

The Corporate Practice Academy is SMU Dedman Law's professional certificate for attorneys already in practice. Home to the nation's Corporate Counsel Externship Program since 2010, Dedman Law has placed attorneys inside 100+ corporate legal departments and understands, better than any institution in America, exactly what those departments need.

Now we're bringing that expertise directly to practicing attorneys at law firms and corporate legal departments across the country — closing the gap between legal expertise and business fluency that separates good lawyers from indispensable ones.

Your attorneys know the law. The question is whether they can do what the business demands.

The Corporate Practice Academy — SMU Dedman School of Law

The curriculum

Four pillars. One stackable credential.

A

Pillar A · Financial Acumen

The Numbers Behind the Business

Read the statements, defend the budget, and speak the CFO's language.

Financial statements & disclosures

Read a balance sheet, income statement, and 10-K with confidence.

Accounting fundamentals

Accrual vs. cash, revenue recognition, and GAAP basics.

Finance principles & metrics

Margins, EBITDA, ROIC, and the cost of capital.

Budgeting & forecasting

Build and defend a legal-department budget to finance leadership.

Capital allocation & deal economics

How the business weighs build, buy, and borrow decisions.

Managing legal spend

Matter budgets, alternative fees, and outside-counsel economics.

B

Pillar B · Substantive Corporate Law

The Corporate Toolkit

Master the legal landscape of corporate life — from the boardroom to the data center.

Corporate governance & board duties

Fiduciary duties, committee structure, and board reporting.

Contract negotiation & drafting

Commercial agreements that allocate real-world risk.

Securities regulation & disclosure

Reporting obligations, Reg FD, and public-company duties.

Data privacy & cybersecurity

Breach response, cross-border data, and vendor risk.

Compliance & regulatory programs

Building programs that withstand regulator scrutiny.

IP, insider trading & enforcement

Protecting core assets; trading policies and 10b5-1 plans.

C

Pillar C · Leadership & Operations

The Trusted Advisor

Lead the department and earn a seat at every important table.

Executive presence & communication

Brief a CEO and a board with clarity and credibility.

Working with the C-suite & board

Committee engagement and board-ready materials.

Crisis management & investigations

Run an internal investigation under real pressure.

Legal department structure & ops

Design, staffing, technology, and legal operations.

Outside counsel selection & management

Choose, manage, and measure your law firms.

Strategic planning & change leadership

Drive change across the organization, not just the team.

D

Pillar D · AI & Technology Governance

The AI-Ready Lawyer

Govern AI with the same fluency you bring to finance, contracts, and the boardroom.

Understanding AI technology & LLMs

How models work, and the vocabulary to challenge vendor claims.

The AI regulatory landscape

TRAIGA, the Colorado AI Act, healthcare and employment AI, and the federal framework.

Internal governance & AI contracting

Governance committees, impact assessments, the NIST framework, and vendor provisions.

AI enablement & strategic leadership

Position legal to accelerate responsible adoption across the business.

Hands-on legal AI platform

Every participant works in a real legal-AI tool throughout the pillar.

Dual-track: firm & in-house

Tailored modules for both law-firm and in-house practice contexts.

Faculty & speakers

Taught by sitting chief legal officers.

These are the CLOs and GCs who lead the legal departments of America's most recognized companies — the experts, and the client. They teach alongside SMU Dedman Law faculty, with the program directed by Stephen B. Yeager, co-author of Inside Counsel.

Amy Yeager

EVP & Chief Legal Officer · Children's Health

David McAtee

SEVP & General Counsel · AT&T

Mark Howard

Chief Legal Officer · Nationwide

Kyle Levine

EVP & Chief Legal Officer · Alaska Airlines

Katie Kane

SVP, General Counsel & Chief Compliance Officer · Texas Instruments

Van Beckwith

EVP & Chief Legal Officer · Halliburton

Chief Counsel, Risk & Regulatory Legal Group · Charles Schwab

Who it's for

Value that lands for firms, companies, and careers.

Law firms

Develop associates who win and keep clients.

  • Build business fluency in high-potential associates
  • Strengthen client service and realization rates
  • Differentiate in recruiting, lateral hiring, and pitches
  • Firm-branded cohorts and bulk enrollment available
Corporate legal departments

A practical alternative to costly executive education.

  • Immediate impact — budgets, board materials, crisis playbooks
  • Direct access to peer CLOs and GCs for benchmarking
  • Ideal for rising leaders and senior in-house counsel
  • Train one attorney or an entire department
Individual attorneys

Accelerate readiness for in-house roles.

  • Shorten the learning curve of corporate practice
  • Build confidence in finance, operations, and the boardroom
  • Credentials and badges that signal client focus
  • Stackable — start with one pillar, build over time
Built to enroll teams Volume discounts to 30%+ Firm-branded cohorts Founding Sponsor program Multi-year incentives
Choose your journey

The Transitioner

Client & Leadership → Financial & Business Fluency → AI & Technology Governance → Corporate Law & Governance

The Rising GC

Financial & Business Fluency → Client & Leadership → AI & Technology Governance → Corporate Law & Governance

The Client-Fluent Associate

Corporate Law & Governance → Client & Leadership → Financial & Business Fluency → AI & Technology Governance

The AI Lead

AI & Technology Governance first, then stack the remaining three in any order

The credential stack

Start with one pillar. Build to all four.

Bronze

Any 1 pillar · 4–6 weeks

  • SMU Dedman Law certificate
  • One Credly digital badge
  • Inside Counsel textbook
  • Residency add-on eligible

Silver

Any 2 pillars · ~10 weeks

  • SMU Dedman Law certificate
  • Two Credly digital badges
  • Inside Counsel textbook
  • Residency add-on eligible

Gold

Any 3 pillars · ~16 weeks

  • SMU Dedman Law certificate
  • Three Credly digital badges
  • Alumni network access
  • Residency add-on eligible
Flagship

Platinum

All 4 pillars · 21 weeks

  • Four Credly digital badges
  • Alumni network + job alerts
  • Substantial CLE credit, including self-study hours through curated readings and exercises — final accreditation per jurisdiction
  • Residency add-on eligible

Every pillar ends in assessed work product — your credential links to a portfolio, not an attendance record.

How it's delivered

Completable online. No travel required.

  • A weekly rhythm built for working lawyers: two or three 2–3-minute video modules, one 45-minute live case session with faculty, and one short step toward your pillar work product — about 90 minutes, total
  • Each pillar runs four to six weeks; take one per quarter or stack all four to Platinum in under six months
  • Substantial CLE credit, including self-study hours through curated readings and exercises — final accreditation per jurisdiction
  • The full credential earned without ever leaving the office
4–6
Weeks per pillar
<6 mo.
To Platinum, back to back
~90 min
Per week, structured
100%
Online — no travel

The business case

Return your organization can measure.

No travel budgets. No lost billing days. Training that directly improves what your attorneys do the morning after they complete it.

Reduce outside counsel dependency

Attorneys who understand finance, governance, and AI handle more substantive matters in-house — and know precisely when escalation is warranted.

Compress the learning curve

Associates reach full corporate-law productivity faster — less time shadowing, more autonomous work, and a faster return on every hire and promotion.

Prevent governance failures

Better-trained counsel makes better AI, data, and compliance decisions. One avoided regulatory misstep can more than offset the cost of the entire program.

What your team keeps after every pillar

  • A legal-department budget — built and defended
  • A board-ready briefing on enterprise risk
  • An AI governance charter and use policy
  • A crisis-response and investigation playbook

The optional intensive residency

One day in Dallas.

  • In the room with sitting CLOs, CFOs, and GCs for hands-on, high-pressure simulation
  • Defend a budget, draft a board memo, lead an investigation, govern an AI deployment
  • Direct benchmarking and peer networking no online program can replicate
  • The full credential is earned without attending — residency is additive, never required

Entirely optional — held once per cohort cycle in Dallas, TX

Join the Faculty

Teach what it actually took.

"Think back to your first year in-house. You knew the law. But nobody taught you how to read a balance sheet, brief a board, or run an investigation when the stakes were real. You figured it out the hard way. The next generation should have it easier."

We're inviting a small, select group of senior in-house leaders to share what they've learned — and help hundreds of attorneys learn it the better way.

Legacy

The attorneys who complete this program will remember who taught them. That kind of legacy outlasts any deal, any case, any title.

Scale

Not one mentee at a time — hundreds of attorneys learning from your experience and judgment across firms and legal departments.

Ease

A pre-recorded session of two to three minutes, built around your strongest topics. No travel. No syllabi. Your story, your voice.

Caliber

You'll be positioned alongside CLOs and GCs whose caliber matches yours — a faculty that reflects your standing in the profession.

Interested in joining this faculty?
